description: After each documented batch, run a CE generated-spec scan for the batch operations and assert none contain generated placeholder descriptions or x-placeholder metadata.
After each documented batch, run the same placeholder scan against the EE generated spec for the batch operations.
Run npm --prefix sdk run openapi:generate and verify CE/legacy JSON/YAML specs are regenerated without script failure.
Run npm --prefix sdk run openapi:generate -- --edition ee and verify EE JSON/YAML specs are regenerated without script failure.
For each new registrar file, verify server/src/lib/api/openapi/index.ts imports and registers it before inventory backfill so manual routes override placeholders.
For representative API-key v1 controllers, inspect one read route and one mutation route per domain group to confirm OpenAPI auth/RBAC/tenant descriptions match source behavior.
For representative billing/financial and invoice routes, verify request body required fields and response envelopes against controller/service/Zod source before committing their registrar entries.
For QuickBooks/integration duplicate route families, verify whether routes are aliases, duplicated controllers, or divergent implementations, and document the distinction in operation descriptions.
For webhook and workflow route families, verify security schemes and callback/event payload schemas against route handlers and service code.
Before each commit, run git status --short and verify .env.localtest is not staged while registrar and generated spec files are staged intentionally.
At final completion, run full CE and EE placeholder scans and assert remaining count is zero in both specs.
At final completion, review /tmp/alga-openapi-doc-progress.md, PRD.md, features.json, tests.json, and SCRATCHPAD.md for consistency with the final route/spec state.
